title: Debits, Credits & Normal Balances
event: Reyes Co.'s Cash T-account shows total debits of $9,000 and total credits of $6,500. Determine the ending balance and its side.

computationPaths.narration: Assets, Expenses, Dividends grow with DEBITS. Liabilities, Equity, Revenues grow with CREDITS. Cash is an asset — its balance sits on the debit side.

memorize.body: DEAD CLIC: Debits increase Expenses, Assets, Dividends · Credits increase Liabilities, Income (revenue), Capital (equity).
'Normal balance' = the side that increases the account, and where its balance almost always lands.
Debit ≠ bad, credit ≠ good. They're just left and right. Revenue is a credit and it's the best news on the page.

questions.prompt: Cash T-account: debits $9,000, credits $6,500. Ending balance = ?

distractors.feedback: You added both sides. Debits and credits OFFSET within an account: 9,000 − 6,500 = 2,500 debit balance.
That's just the credit total. Net the two sides against each other.
